name: VMware Workspace ONE
description: Monitor device compliance, enrollment status, and user access in VMware Workspace ONE UEM (Unified Endpoint Management).

config.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to your Workspace ONE UEM console
2. Go to Groups & Settings > All Settings > System > Advanced > API > REST API
3. Enable API access if not already enabled
4. Copy the API Key (aw-tenant-code) from the General tab
5. Create or use an admin account with API role access
6. Enter the console URL (e.g. https://as1234.awmdm.com), username, password, and API key below

credentialFields.label: Tenant URL
credentialFields.helpText: Your Workspace ONE UEM console URL (e.g. https://as1234.awmdm.com)

credentialFields.label: API Username
credentialFields.helpText: Admin username with REST API access in Workspace ONE UEM

credentialFields.label: API Password
credentialFields.helpText: Password for the API admin user

credentialFields.label: API Key (aw-tenant-code)
credentialFields.helpText: REST API key from Groups & Settings > All Settings > System > Advanced > API > REST API

checks.name: Secure Devices
checks.description: Verify enrolled devices are compliant and not compromised in Workspace ONE UEM

checks.name: Device List
checks.description: Retrieve and verify all enrolled devices in Workspace ONE UEM for asset inventory

checks.name: Employee Access
checks.description: Verify user accounts and their enrollment status in Workspace ONE UEM
